title: Before we ship a Claude model, these teams try to break it.
summary: A video from Anthropic describing their internal adversarial testing process for Claude models before release. It covers the teams and processes responsible for quality assurance, stress-testing, and feedback integration in model development.
writeup: A short documentary segment in which Anthropic early-access customers describe what it is like to receive and stress-test a new Claude model before public release. The through-line is how automated evals, not human intuition, are the first gate: customers watch eval pass rates climb in real time and read that signal as a proxy for what the next generation of models will improve. A concrete anchor is the S1 drafting use case, where agentic capabilities now let a model range across source material, synthesize, and edit large document sections autonomously, collapsing a task that was once a pipe dream into something routinely delegated.
key_points: Automated evals are the first thing customers run when a new model arrives, with results streaming in the background so the team has a quantitative baseline before any qualitative impression forms.

One customer reported a 20% jump in testing-agent success rate simply by swapping in the new model, moving the agent from inconsistently answering questions to answering every question quickly and accurately.

Drafting an S1, a highly complex multi-part legal document, is cited as the clearest proof-of-concept for agentic capabilities: the model ranges out to find needed information, synthesizes it, and edits document sections, handling progressively larger chunks autonomously.

Evals that have never passed before suddenly passing, and passing consistently, is described as the primary leading indicator that a model will be something special, more diagnostic than any single benchmark.

The relationship between Anthropic and these customers is characterized as co-building rather than vendor-customer: engineers from both sides collaborate closely, and the customers maintain a high-trust bar precisely because Anthropic publishes carefully rather than shipping slop.

The compounding dynamic these builders describe: receiving the latest tools improves their product, which raises customer expectations, which in turn pulls the next round of model improvements forward.

chapters.title: Agentic S1 Drafting and Success Rate Jump
